title: CLI subcommand simplification: de-emphasize bootstrap/config/hooks in help
description: Mark bootstrap, config, and hooks commands as hidden in the CLI help output so 'fabric --help' leads with init as the primary entry. Citty does not natively support meta.hidden on subcommands; if no built-in mechanism exists, move them under a 'fabric advanced' wrapper group or annotate them in help text. All three commands must remain callable directly — no breaking change. Update README docs to reflect new primary flow.

files.change: Investigate citty API for hidden subcommand support; if supported, add meta.hidden: true to bootstrap, config, hooks entries; if not, consider grouping them under an 'advanced' subcommand wrapper.
If citty supports grouping or hiding, wire the advanced subcommands accordingly; ensure 'fabric bootstrap install', 'fabric config install', 'fabric hooks install' remain callable without error.
Move bootstrap/config/hooks usage into an 'Advanced commands' section; add a note that these are auto-run by 'fabric init' and rarely needed standalone.
Mirror README.md changes — emphasize 'fabric init' as the one-stop initialization entry in the quick-start section.

implementation: Check citty source or docs for subcommand hidden support: look for meta.hidden in citty's TypeScript types or in its readme. If supported, set meta: { hidden: true } on bootstrap, config, and hooks in allCommands.

If citty does not support hidden, create an 'advanced' wrapper command in index.ts that groups bootstrap/config/hooks as its subcommands; register 'advanced' in allCommands; keep the original top-level bootstrap/config/hooks entries removed from allCommands but still importable.
Whichever approach: run 'fabric --help' and confirm bootstrap/config/hooks are not in the main command list; run 'fabric bootstrap install --help' and confirm it still works.
Update README.md: add an '## Advanced Commands' section after the Quick Start section that briefly documents bootstrap/config/hooks with a note they are auto-run by init.
Update packages/cli/README.md with the same structural change.
Run pnpm test to confirm no command routing regressions.

reference.pattern: citty allCommands registry; explore citty's CommandMeta type for hidden flag support before committing to wrapper approach
reference.examples: exploration-patterns.json (citty_subcommand_registration): 'The key becomes the CLI subcommand name. There is no alias/multi-name mechanism in citty — rename is atomic'; need to verify hidden support separately

anchor_context: exploration-patterns.json (citty_subcommand_registration section) — citty feature set notes

criteria: 'fabric --help' USAGE block does not list bootstrap, config, or hooks as top-level commands (hidden or grouped under advanced)
'fabric bootstrap install --help' exits 0 and shows correct usage (no breaking change)
README.md quick-start section leads with 'fabric init'; bootstrap/config/hooks appear only under Advanced Commands

rationale.chosen_approach: Use citty's meta.hidden if available; fall back to 'fabric advanced' wrapper if not
alternatives_considered: Simply update README without touching CLI help — incomplete; user expects help text to reflect init as primary
Remove top-level commands entirely — breaking change; user said 'no breaking change'
decision_factors: User said: 'other commands should be hidden/de-emphasized, kept as internal modules'
No breaking change constraint means commands must remain callable
Citty hidden support is the cleanest solution if available
rationale.tradeoffs: If citty lacks hidden support, wrapper approach adds one extra command level ('fabric advanced bootstrap install'); acceptable per constraint

manual_checks: fabric --help: bootstrap, config, hooks NOT in top-level command list
fabric config install --clients=claude --dry-run: still works (regression from IMPL-002)
success_metrics: Help text change visible after pnpm build
No existing command routing tests broken
